task_completion.description: Verify that the window and level values have been changed from their initial state.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for text on the screen (often in the corners of the image viewer) showing Window/Level (W/L or WW/WL) values. They should no longer be the initial values of W: 400 and L: 40.

task_completion.description: Verify that the CT image visually reflects a lung window.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the main CT image. The large lung areas should be dark gray/black with fine white branching structures (blood vessels and airways) clearly visible, rather than being completely pitch black as in a soft-tissue window.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Window Width (W or WW) is adjusted to a standard lung window range.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Window Width (W or WW) value on the screen overlay or settings panel and verify it is between 1000 and 2000 HU.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Window Level (L or WL) is adjusted to a standard lung window range.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Window Level (L or WL) value on the screen overlay or settings panel and verify it is a negative value between -400 and -800 HU.

task_completion.description: Verify that the optimal target window and level settings were achieved exactly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the exact text 'W: 1500' (or WW: 1500) and 'L: -600' (or WL: -600) in the viewer's overlay or settings panel.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the medical imaging viewer's GUI rather than using terminal commands or scripts to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the active medical imaging application window, mouse cursor interacting with the image (e.g., dragging to adjust window/level) or UI panels, and absence of terminal-based scripting.

integrity.description: Verify that the visual appearance of the CT scan corresponds to the displayed window/level values.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the main image area is an actual medical scan that visually updates to look like a lung window, confirming the agent didn't just edit a text overlay or use a disconnected text box.

integrity.description: Verify that the correct patient scan is being manipulated.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for 'LIDC-IDRI-0001' in the patient information overlay (usually in the top-left or top-right corner of the scan) to ensure a stock image wasn't loaded.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Patient ID: LIDC-IDRI-0001. Initial settings: Window Width (W) = 400, Window Level (L) = 40. Target optimal lung window settings: Window Width (W) = 1500, Window Level (L) = -600.
